goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Hey Ameena. You said you're feeling at 68 this morning, and that matches what I'm seeing — a tough start.

**Let's start with sleep:** last night was critically short at 4.4 hours, with a very late bedtime around 5:49 AM. That's a full hour less than your recent average, and it's part of a downward trend. The good news is your body's recovery system is still strong — your HRV was high at 119.6, which shows real resilience even when you're stretched thin.

**What's happening:** yesterday you were under extreme stress (82/100) with very low coping capacity (36). That stress, combined with the severe sleep debt and a nutritional deficit (only 868 calories), creates a compounding strain — your body's resources are being drained from multiple directions at once. Your activity was also extremely low, with zero moderate exercise, which fits the picture of a day where everything felt overwhelming.

**What's working:** your physiological recovery is holding up remarkably well despite all this. That's a real strength — your autonomic system is still giving you a buffer.

Given all this, today is about **containing the strain.** You need to protect your evening for sleep, find micro-moments to ease the tension that's been building, and give your body the fuel it's missing.

The theme today is creating small buffers against that compounding pressure. Your evening wind-down and protein focus are direct responses to the sleep and nutrition gaps.

Even with a rough night, your recovery system is still fighting for you — today's about giving it the support it needs.

assumptions: User is observing Ramadan (meal descriptions indicate suhoor/iftar)
Timezone is Asia/Dubai (bundle meta)
User has access to phone/alarm for external cues
User can adjust evening schedule to some degree
User has control over caffeine intake timing

sleep_debt_evidence: gap_today 17 (overall > proxy, mismatch run length 4 days)

soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
caution with late caffeine given sleep fragmentation risk
consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend
follow-up on missing goals data (goal confidence is 0)
acknowledge low nutrition confidence (0.76) when interpreting intake

generated_actions.title: Set a 6:30 PM alarm to start a 90-minute screen-free wind-down
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's sleep was critically short (4.4h) with a very late bedtime (~5:49 AM) and sleep duration is declining (slope_fast -0.16). Given today's priority 'sleep_consistency_recovery', the risk flag 'sleep_debt', and the soft constraint 'prioritize sleep over additional activity', an extremely early wind-down start creates a protected buffer to aim for earlier sleep onset and longer duration tonight.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 6:30 PM alarm help you start a longer, earlier wind-down tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1200) AND user reports using the alarm to initiate a screen-free wind-down routine.

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: When your alarm goes off at 6:30 PM, stop all work, dim the lights, and begin a 90-minute pre-sleep routine: no screens, gentle tidying, reading, or stretching. Aim to be in bed by 8:00 PM to maximize sleep opportunity.

must_avoid: starting new tasks after the alarm

generated_actions.title: When you feel 'Tense' during the day, text or think of one person you appreciate
generated_actions.rationale: Stability status is 'poor' with extremely high stress (82, +1.86 fast z-score) rising sharply (slope_fast +8.39) and very poor coping capacity (36, -1.62 fast z-score) declining. Emotions included 'Tense'. Leveraging your strong social wellbeing (88) as a resource via a micro-intervention can disrupt negative emotional spirals and address the 'volatility' and 'burnout_risk' flags.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the social gratitude pause when you felt tense today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the brief social gratitude technique at least once when tension or stress arose.

assumptions: You can take brief pauses
You have access to your phone or thoughts
generated_actions.description: Yesterday you reported feeling 'Tense' amid extremely high stress (82) and low coping (36). When that feeling arises today, pause for 1 minute and send a quick appreciation text to a friend/family member, or simply think of someone you're grateful for.

must_avoid: continuing the stressful task without pause

generated_actions.title: Add an extra protein source to your Iftar meal tonight
generated_actions.rationale: Nutrition status is 'critical' with severe caloric deficit (868 kcal, -1.02 fast z-score) and low protein (49.8g, -0.71 fast z-score), both declining sharply (protein slope_fast -9.98, calories slope_fast -249.6). The risk flag 'nutrition_gap' and soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits' indicate reinforcing protein intake today to support energy needs and recovery.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you intentionally add extra protein to your Iftar meal tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 60g (improving on yesterday's 49.8g) AND user reports consciously enhancing their evening meal.

assumptions: You are preparing Iftar
You have access to additional protein sources
generated_actions.description: When preparing your Ramadan Iftar meal, consciously include an additional protein source beyond the rabbit meat—like extra lentils, chickpeas, a hard-boiled egg, or a scoop of yogurt—to boost total intake.

generated_actions.title: Do 5 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing immediately after getting into bed
generated_actions.rationale: Stress was extremely high yesterday (82) and rising sharply (slope_fast +8.39), while sleep duration was critically short (4.4h). The cross-domain signal 'high_stress_with_sleep_debt_and_low_nutrition' suggests stress may interfere with sleep onset. This intentional breathing reset creates a calm transition into sleep, directly supporting your sleep goal domain and addressing the 'volatility' risk flag.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete the breathing exercise immediately after getting into bed tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports doing the breathing reset before any other bedtime activity.

assumptions: You can lie quietly in bed
generated_actions.description: When you physically get into bed tonight, before reaching for your phone or any other activity, complete 5 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing: inhale 4 seconds, hold 7 seconds, exhale 8 seconds. Focus only on your breath.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Good morning Ameena — you're reporting perfect energy this morning, which is fantastic to hear.

Your sleep last night was short at 5.2 hours — that's below your usual and continues a declining trend. What's really striking is the gap between how you feel and what your body is showing: your physiological recovery is critically low (HRV at 58.2, proxy score 32), while your subjective energy is high (84). This is the third day in a row of this mismatch, which suggests you might be running on nervous system activation rather than true fuel. Yesterday's high stress (74) plus the short sleep means your body didn't get the deep recovery it needed, and that's compounding with the extremely low activity (3,821 steps) and reduced calorie intake (1,130 kcal) from your Ramadan fasting. When stress stays elevated and nutrition/activity drop, the strain on your system builds fast.

The bright spot is your strong coping capacity (86) — even with the emotional strain, you're reporting the ability to manage it. That resilience is something real to build on today.

Given all this, today is about gentle recovery and protecting your energy stores. Your actions focus on winding down much earlier tonight and adding nourishment after your fast breaks.

You've got the coping strength — today's about pairing that with the physical support your body is asking for.

evidence_quotes: I was working on an incident report related to IRB approval

meal_descriptions.description: 2 table spoons of fava beans and 2 fried eggs and 3 dates with 1 Arabic bread loaf
2 spoons of rice with molokheya and rabbit meat and mix vegetable salad.
